---
title: "NFL Models"
author: "AJ King & Will Eberhardt"
date: "2026-05-05"
output: pdf_document
---
```{r, include = FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(knitr)
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = "/Users/wille/Desktop/STATS/STAT333_WillProject")
nfl_data <- read.csv("https://data.scorenetwork.org/data/nfl-team-statistics.csv")
str(nfl_data)

nfl_data <- nfl_data %>%
  filter(season >= 2006)


names(nfl_data)
```



```{r}
nfl_model_data <- nfl_data %>%
  mutate(
    win_pct = (wins + 0.5 * ties) / (wins + losses + ties),
    winning_record = ifelse(win_pct >= 0.5, 1, 0),
    turnovers_bad = offense_n_interceptions +
      offense_n_fumbles_lost_pass +
      offense_n_fumbles_lost_run,
    turnovers_good = defense_n_interceptions +
      defense_n_fumbles_lost_pass +
      defense_n_fumbles_lost_run,
    turnover_diff = turnovers_bad - turnovers_good
  ) %>%
  arrange(team, season) %>%
  group_by(team) %>%
  mutate(
    next_winning_record = lead(winning_record),
    last_year_winning_record = lag(winning_record),
    last_year_wins = lag(wins)
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  filter(!is.na(next_winning_record))

library(dplyr)
library(knitr)
library(tidyr)

summary_table <- nfl_model_data %>%
  select(
    win_pct,
    last_year_winning_record,
    last_year_wins,
    turnover_diff,
    score_differential,
    next_winning_record
  ) %>%
  summarise(
    across(
      everything(),
      list(
        Mean = ~mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE),
        SD = ~sd(.x, na.rm = TRUE),
        Min = ~min(.x, na.rm = TRUE),
        Max = ~max(.x, na.rm = TRUE)
      )
    )
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = everything(),
    names_to = c("Variable", ".value"),
    names_sep = "_(?=[^_]+$)"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Variable = case_when(
      Variable == "win_pct" ~ "Winning Percentage",
      Variable == "last_year_winning_record" ~ "Winning Record Last Season",
      Variable == "last_year_wins" ~ "Wins Last Season",
      Variable == "turnover_diff" ~ "Turnover Difference",
      Variable == "score_differential" ~ "Score Differential",
      Variable == "next_winning_record" ~ "Winning Record Next Season",
      TRUE ~ Variable
    ),
    across(where(is.numeric), ~round(.x, 3))
  )

kable(
  summary_table,
  caption = "Summary statistics for key NFL model variables.",
  col.names = c("Variable", "Mean", "SD", "Min", "Max"),
  align = "lcccc"
)

```
This makes winning records for win count over 9. Makes a turnover statistic.
```{r}
summary(nfl_model_data$next_winning_record)
table(nfl_model_data$next_winning_record)

```
Now we get to the models
```{r}
model1 <- glm(
  next_winning_record ~ win_pct,
  data = nfl_model_data,
  family = binomial
)

summary(model1)

```

```{r}
model2 <- glm(
  next_winning_record ~ win_pct +
    offense_ave_air_yards +
    turnover_diff,
  data = nfl_model_data,
  family = binomial
)

summary(model2)
```

```{r}
model3 <- glm(
  next_winning_record ~ win_pct +
    offense_ave_air_yards+
    turnover_diff +
    score_differential,
  data = nfl_model_data,
  family = binomial
)

summary(model3)
```


```{r}
nfl_model_data <- nfl_model_data %>%
  mutate(
    pred_prob_model1 = predict(model1, type = "response"),
    pred_class_model1 = ifelse(pred_prob_model1 >= 0.5, 1, 0),
    
    pred_prob_model2 = predict(model2, type = "response"),
    pred_class_model2 = ifelse(pred_prob_model2 >= 0.5, 1, 0),
    
    pred_prob_model3 = predict(model3, type = "response"),
    pred_class_model3 = ifelse(pred_prob_model3 >= 0.5, 1, 0)
  )

```

```{r}
accuracy_model1 <- mean(nfl_model_data$pred_class_model1 == nfl_model_data$next_winning_record)
accuracy_model2 <- mean(nfl_model_data$pred_class_model2 == nfl_model_data$next_winning_record)
accuracy_model3 <- mean(nfl_model_data$pred_class_model3 == nfl_model_data$next_winning_record)

accuracy_table <- data.frame(
  Model = c("Model 1", "Model 2", "Model 3"),
  Accuracy = c(accuracy_model1, accuracy_model2, accuracy_model3)
)

kable(accuracy_table)

```



```{r}
accuracy_graph_data <- data.frame(
  Model = c("Model 1", "Model 2", "Model 3"),
  Accuracy_Percent = c(62.1, 63.1, 65.2)
)

accuracy_graph_data$Model <- factor(
  accuracy_graph_data$Model,
  levels = c("Model 1", "Model 2", "Model 3")
)

accuracy_plot <- ggplot(
  accuracy_graph_data,
  aes(x = Model, y = Accuracy_Percent, fill = Model)
) +
  geom_col(width = 0.65) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = paste0(Accuracy_Percent, "%")),
    vjust = -0.5,
    size = 5
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(50, 70)) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = seq(50, 70, 5),
    labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Model 1" = "#d2b48c",
      "Model 2" = "#a97142",
      "Model 3" = "#5c3317"
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Model Accuracy Comparison",
    x = "Model",
    y = "Accuracy"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  )

accuracy_plot
```

```{r}
table(
  Predicted = nfl_model_data$pred_class_model1,
  Actual = nfl_model_data$next_winning_record
)

table(
  Predicted = nfl_model_data$pred_class_model2,
  Actual = nfl_model_data$next_winning_record
)

table(
  Predicted = nfl_model_data$pred_class_model3,
  Actual = nfl_model_data$next_winning_record
)

```

```{r}
AIC(model1, model2, model3)
```

```{r}
model_comparison <- data.frame(
  Model = c("Model 1: Previous winning record",
            "Model 2: Offense + turnovers",
            "Model 3: Offense + turnovers + score differential"),
  AIC = c(AIC(model1), AIC(model2), AIC(model3))
)

kable(
  model_comparison,
  caption = "Model comparison using AIC. Lower AIC indicates a better-fitting model."
)
```

```{r}
odds_ratios <- data.frame(
  Variable = names(coef(model3)),
  Estimate = coef(model3)
)

kable(
  odds_ratios,
  digits = 3,
  caption = "Estimated coefficient for Model 3."
)
```

```{r}
nfl_model_data <- nfl_model_data %>%
  mutate(
    pred_prob_m1 = predict(model1, type = "response"),
    pred_class_m1 = ifelse(pred_prob_m1 >= 0.5, 1, 0),
    
    pred_prob_m2 = predict(model2, type = "response"),
    pred_class_m2 = ifelse(pred_prob_m2 >= 0.5, 1, 0),
    
    pred_prob_m3 = predict(model3, type = "response"),
    pred_class_m3 = ifelse(pred_prob_m3 >= 0.5, 1, 0)
  )

accuracy_table <- data.frame(
  Model = c("Model 1", "Model 2", "Model 3"),
  Accuracy = c(
    mean(nfl_model_data$pred_class_m1 == nfl_model_data$next_winning_record),
    mean(nfl_model_data$pred_class_m2 == nfl_model_data$next_winning_record),
    mean(nfl_model_data$pred_class_m3 == nfl_model_data$next_winning_record)
  )
)

kable(
  accuracy_table,
  digits = 3,
  caption = "Classification accuracy for each model using a 0.50 probability cutoff."
)
```

```{r}
ggplot(nfl_model_data, aes(x = last_year_wins, y = wins)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  labs(
    title = "Current Season Wins vs. Previous Season Wins",
    x = "Previous Season Wins",
    y = "Current Season Wins"
  )
```


```{r}
actual_2023 <- read.csv("/Users/wille/Desktop/STATS/STAT333_WillProject/2023NFL_WL.csv") %>%

  select(season, team, wins, losses, ties) %>%
  mutate(
    actual_win_pct_2023 = (wins + 0.5 * ties) / (wins + losses + ties),
    actual_winning_record_2023 = ifelse(actual_win_pct_2023 >= 0.5, 1, 0)
  )


```


```{r}
actual_2023 <- read.csv("/Users/wille/Desktop/STATS/STAT333_WillProject/2023NFL_WL.csv") %>%
  select(season, team, wins, losses, ties) %>%
  mutate(
    actual_win_pct_2023 = (wins + 0.5 * ties) / (wins + losses + ties),
    actual_winning_record_2023 = ifelse(actual_win_pct_2023 >= 0.5, 1, 0)
  )

predict_2023_data <- nfl_data %>%
  filter(season == 2022) %>%
  mutate(
    win_pct = (wins + 0.5 * ties) / (wins + losses + ties),
    turnovers_bad = offense_n_interceptions +
      offense_n_fumbles_lost_pass +
      offense_n_fumbles_lost_run,
    turnovers_good = defense_n_interceptions +
      defense_n_fumbles_lost_pass +
      defense_n_fumbles_lost_run,
    turnover_diff = turnovers_bad - turnovers_good
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    pred_prob_2023 = predict(model3, newdata = ., type = "response"),
    predicted_winning_record_2023 = ifelse(pred_prob_2023 >= 0.5, 1, 0)
  ) %>%
  select(team, pred_prob_2023, predicted_winning_record_2023)

prediction_2023_results <- predict_2023_data %>%
  left_join(actual_2023, by = "team") %>%
  mutate(
    Prediction_Result = ifelse(
      predicted_winning_record_2023 == actual_winning_record_2023,
      "Correct",
      "Incorrect"
    )
  )
print(prediction_2023_results)
```



```{r}
prediction_summary <- prediction_2023_results %>%
  count(Prediction_Result) %>%
  mutate(
    Percent = n / sum(n) * 100
  )

ggplot(prediction_summary, aes(x = Prediction_Result, y = n, fill = Prediction_Result)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.65) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = paste0(n, " teams\n", round(Percent, 1), "%")),
    vjust = -0.3,
    size = 5
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Correct" = "#5c3317",
      "Incorrect" = "#d2b48c"
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "2023 Prediction Results Using Model 3",
    x = "",
    y = "Number of Teams"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  )

```
